title: lycopus europaeus var. argutus
intro: Lycopus europaeus var. argutus, also known as European bugle or bugleweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal properties, due to the presence of glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of bugleweed include reducing anxiety and stress, regulating thyroid function, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Bugleweed is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusible herbal supplement, and can be used externally to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
